decisions.title: Prioritize Explore V2 (E2) GA blockers over new features
decisions.context: Multiple E2 bugs surfacing (UI lockups, sharelink issues, chart refresh). GA timeline at risk if not addressed. Signal: ENG-10024, ENG-9721, ENG-6386, ENG-9717.

arguments.for: E2 GA is Q2 commitment. Stability issues erode customer trust. Embedding deeper requires solid foundation.
arguments.against: Feature development (Python Rules, Canvas updates) also drives moat-building. Could slow innovation velocity.

decisions.title: Migrate legacy Playground to Playground-2 architecture now
decisions.context: ENG-8070 suggests major migration underway. Half-finished migrations create tech debt and confusion. Signal: ENG-8070, ENG-10060.

arguments.for: Reduces maintenance burden. Enables faster feature velocity. Aligns with 'Build the Moat' via platform consolidation.
arguments.against: Resource-intensive. May delay customer-facing features. Risk of introducing regressions during migration.

decisions.title: Consolidate client-side parsing strategy (Parquet/TDMS)
decisions.context: Multiple parallel efforts on file parsing: ENG-10057 (Parquet client-side), ENG-7822 (TDMS migration), ENG-9281 (HDF5 CLI), ENG-10061 (HDF5 Frontend). Strategy unclear.
decisions.blockedBy: No clear owner for unified file ingestion roadmap. Risk of duplicate work across Parquet/TDMS/HDF5.

arguments.for: Unified approach enables better Canvas integration (Embed Deeper). Reduces engineering fragmentation. Supports Ask SIFT data access.
arguments.against: Each format has unique requirements. Flexibility allows experimentation. Consolidation may slow near-term delivery.

decisions.title: Ship SCIM sync health visibility to improve enterprise adoption
decisions.context: ENG-9926 directly supports Embed Deeper pillar. Enterprise customers need admin visibility. Low-effort, high-impact for deployment health.

arguments.for: Reduces support burden. Increases enterprise confidence. Directly supports deployment expansion (Decisions Captured metric).
arguments.against: Not a blocker for new customer acquisition. Could prioritize features that drive new use cases instead.

decisions.title: Define feature flag deprecation policy
decisions.context: Multiple flags being removed (ENG-10058, ENG-9951, ENG-9995). Flag proliferation creates tech debt. Need systematic cleanup process.

arguments.for: Reduces complexity. Improves code maintainability. Aligns with Nail the Core (platform hygiene).
arguments.against: Low urgency compared to customer-facing work. Engineers already removing flags organically.

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load (80% of time). Focus blocks fragmented. Consider batching 1:1s to create contiguous deep work time.
alerts: Thursday has 7 hours of back-to-back meetings with no breaks
